source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Product Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
Netic is the AI revenue engine for essential services who are the backbone of the American economy.

With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.

You’ll join our team with relentless builders from Scale, Databricks, HRT, Meta, MIT, Stanford, and Harvard in bringing frontier AI to the physical economy, where the problems are hard, the data is complex, and the impact is immediate and tangible.

As a founding Product Infrastructure Engineer, you’ll architect and scale the backbone supporting our autonomous AI agents—tackling real-world challenges with immediate, tangible impact. You’ll collaborate with a driven team of builders to shape infrastructure and processes from the ground up, leveraging cutting-edge cloud and orchestration technologies. If you thrive in fast-paced, ambiguous environments and are excited to set new standards in the agentic space, this is your opportunity to build and leave your mark.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build cloud infrastructure: Design and operate the backbone that hosts our AI agents and supports our platform.

 - Automate operations: Create infrastructure as code and automated deployment pipelines for reliable releases.

 - Enable scale: Implement systems that handle usage spikes gracefully through autoscaling and multi-region support.

 - Create observability: Build monitoring, logging, and dashboards that provide real-time visibility into system health.

 - Maintain security: Implement security best practices including IAM, network segmentation, and audit trails.


WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Infrastructure experience: 4+ years running distributed systems at scale with a major cloud platforms (we use GCP but AWS and Azure is great, too).

 - Automation skills: Proven record of owning infrastructure-as-code and CI/CD pipelines (Terraform, Git Actions, etc).

 - Performance expertise: Experience optimizing systems and databases to meet latency and cost targets under multi-modal workloads. For example, experience with pgBouncer, Kubernetes-based autoscaling, and similar tools.

 - Observability knowledge: Fluent with modern monitoring and tracing tooling (we use Datadog) and built-in tools in Vercel or GCP.

 - Security awareness: Understanding of enterprise security requirements and compliance needs like authentication and service proxies.

 - Product mindset: Treat infrastructure as a product and prioritize ambiguous requirements to see around the corner for 1-2 years ahead of our current systems—measure impact and iterate continuously.

What brings us together is our commitment to:

 - Live to build

 - Run through walls and win

 - Obsess over customers in each line of code

 - Lose sleep over the "almost perfect"

 - Show internal locus of control

 - Prioritize finesse: refinement of first principles thinking, execution, and craftsmanship

We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Product
source_jobs.description:
Netic is the AI revenue engine for essential services who are the backbone of the American economy.

With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.

Product Engineers at Netic build the features that put AI in the hands of real businesses. This role combines technical excellence with customer empathy, crafting products that make complex AI feel simple and delivering immediate value to essential service industries.



What You'll Do:

 - Build agentic products: Design, code, and ship full-stack features for Netic's AI platform.

 - Co‑create with customers: Work closely with our customers to understand workflows, gather feedback, and turn pain points into features that stick.

 - Own end‑to‑end delivery: Drive projects from spec to production, handling data models, APIs, front‑end polish, and post‑launch iteration.

 - Ensure quality: Build, test, and monitor features in production to ensure reliability and performance.



What You'll Bring:

 - Proven product builder: 2+ years delivering user‑facing software at scale; you’ve carried features through multiple release cycles.

 - Full‑stack fluency: Comfortable with React, TypeScript, Python; experience with databases and cloud infrastructure.

 - AI experience (nice‑to‑have): Hands‑on with LLM APIs, embeddings, or RAG patterns—or hunger to learn fast.

 - Customer empathy: Track record of translating stakeholder goals into crisp technical specs and delightful products.

 - Founder‑level ownership: You run toward ambiguity, write clean code quickly, and see failures as data for the next sprint.

 - Excellent communicator: Able to explain architectural trade‑offs to eng and business outcomes to execs.

What brings us together is our commitment to:

 - Live to build

 - Run through walls and win

 - Obsess over customers in each line of code

 - Lose sleep over the "almost perfect"

 - Show internal locus of control

 - Prioritize finesse: refinement of first principles thinking, execution, and craftsmanship

We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.title: Applied AI Research Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Netic is the AI revenue engine for essential services who are the backbone of the American economy.

With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.

You’ll join our team with relentless builders from Scale, Databricks, HRT, Meta, MIT, Stanford, and Harvard in bringing frontier AI to the physical economy, where the problems are hard, the data is complex, and the impact is immediate and tangible.

As an Applied AI Research Engineer, you’ll dive deep into cutting-edge research, understand the business functions we put on autopilot inside-out, and execute targeted ML projects that deliver pure magic.



What You'll Do:

 - Study the frontier: Track frontier work in traditional ML, LLMs, multimodal models, retrieval, and agentic systems—then distill it into ideas we can ship.

 - Identify high‑ROI projects: Partner with GTM and ops teams to spot bottlenecks in products; define ML projects that unlock significant leverage for customers.

 - Build targeted models: Own the full cycle—data curation, training, evaluation, and deployment—delivering systems that solve real customer pain points.

 - Productionize solutions: Integrate models into our real-time platform via robust APIs and streaming pipelines, ensuring model performance and guardrails from day one.

 - Self‑direct & ship: Operate like a founder—set technical roadmap, validate quickly, and iterate based on real-world results.



What You'll Bring:

 - Deep ML experience: 4+ years with cutting-edge ML techniques; fluent in PyTorch or JAX and modern serving frameworks.

 - Research‑to‑revenue record: Proof you’ve taken novel ML concepts from paper → prod with measurable $$ impact or user growth.

 - Full‑stack pragmatism: Comfortable with ETL, feature stores, cloud-native infrastructure, and A/B experimentation.

 - Data engineering skills: Experience working with complex, real-world data streams and building reliable training pipelines.

 - Product intuition: Ability to understand customer workflows and translate business needs into technical solutions.

 - Ownership model: You default to action, uphold a high craftsmanship bar, and treat failure modes as learning‑rate multipliers.

What brings us together is our commitment to:

 - Live to build

 - Run through walls and win

 - Obsess over customers in each line of code

 - Lose sleep over the "almost perfect"

 - Close the feedback loop: Feed market insights back to product and engineering so we ship the features that unblock larger contracts.

What You’ll Do:

 - Own full‑cycle hiring: Drive sourcing → close for engineers, ML researchers, and GTM technologists; you’re the single threaded owner on every job req.

 - Build outbound machinery: Design repeatable email, LinkedIn, and community hunts that surface 10× builders long before they’re on the market.

 - Craft the right filters: Build out role‑specific rubrics, technical screens, and onsite loops that predict on‑the‑job success.

 - Sell the mission: Deliver a crisp, credible pitch on Netic’s frontier‑AI moat that converts “maybe” candidates into same‑day signers.

 - Instrument & iterate: Track funnel metrics, time‑to‑hire, and close rates; turn data into weekly process tweaks that keep the bar high and the pipeline fast.



What You’ll Bring:

 - Sourcing excellence: Demonstrated top tier sourced‑> hire ratio and mastery of advanced search, referral loops, and niche communities.

 - Process architect chops: Proven ability to spin up scalable interview frameworks and ATS automations without drowning candidates in steps.

 - Storytelling edge: Track record turning complex tech visions into narratives that close passive candidates against big research lab‑level offers.

source_jobs.title: Content & Storytelling Wizard
source_jobs.description:
Netic is the AI revenue engine for essential services who are the backbone of the American economy.

With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.

You’ll join our team with relentless builders from Scale, Databricks, HRT, Meta, MIT, Stanford, and Harvard in bringing frontier AI to the physical economy, where the problems are hard, the data is complex, and the impact is immediate and tangible.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Netic serves the most essential businesses in America, and each one has a story worth telling.

The family-owned HVAC company that has served its community for decades.

The pest control business that won its first customers through grit and ingenuity.

The wellness center finding new ways to grow membership and keep people coming back.

In this Content & Storytelling role, you’ll bring those stories to life.

You’ll create videos, written content, and other storytelling assets that show how essential businesses use AI to grow revenue, operate more effectively, and deliver a better customer experience. You’ll spotlight the people behind these businesses, turn their wins into compelling narratives, and create content that helps others learn from them.

This is an exciting opportunity for someone who wants to tell the stories of the businesses that keep the real world running.




YOU WILL

 - Tell the stories of Netic customers and industry leaders across video, articles, social content, and more, turning the people we feature into heroes.

 - Own Netic’s organic social presence and help build the playbook for how we show up across Facebook, LinkedIn, YouTube, and other channels.

 - Create helpful, engaging content such as email newsletters, product guides, and educational resources that help businesses get more value from Netic.

 - Support go-to-market efforts with high-impact sales and marketing assets, including case studies, one-pagers, and other enablement materials.

What brings us together is our commitment to:

 - Live to build

 - Run through walls and win

 - Obsess over customers in each line of code

 - Lose sleep over the "almost perfect"

 - Show internal locus of control

 - Prioritize finesse: refinement of first principles thinking, execution, and craftsmanship

We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.title: Founding Field & Event Marketer
source_jobs.description:
Netic is the AI revenue engine for essential services who are the backbone of the American economy.

With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.

You’ll join our team with relentless builders from Scale, Databricks, HRT, Meta, MIT, Stanford, and Harvard in bringing frontier AI to the physical economy, where the problems are hard, the data is complex, and the impact is immediate and tangible.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Netic sells into the real world. Our best customers run businesses where trust is earned in person, relationships compound over time, and the biggest opportunities show up when you’re in the room.

That’s where you come in.

As our Founding Field & Event Marketer, you’ll build one of the most critical parts of Netic’s go-to-market engine from scratch. This is a rare chance to define how a category-defining AI company shows up in person and turn real-world interactions into a durable growth engine.

You’ll create the playbooks that bring in new buyers, deepen our partner ecosystem, and turn customers into raving fans. You’ll own our presence at major tradeshows, regional events, and Netic-hosted programs, delivering experiences that feel premium, convert demand, and build lasting customer love.

We’re looking for someone who’s equal parts visionary and operator. You’ll define what “Netic in the real world” feels like and sweat the details that make us unforgettable.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Partner with Sales to bring prospects into and down the funnel. Build programs around the right accounts and territories, set a high bar for execution, and equip AEs with playbooks and tools to win in their territories.

 - Own the end-to-end field and events playbook. Lead strategy and execution across major tradeshows, sponsored activations, regional meetups, and Netic-hosted events, delivering standout customer experiences and strong ROI every time.

 - Own performance and outcomes. Define goals, track results, and run post-event retros to optimize every program. You’ll be accountable for the full health of your program.

 - Bring high levels of taste and operational excellence. Pair sharp creative instincts with relentless attention to detail, from concept to run-of-show, so every touchpoint feels premium and the trains run on time.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5+ years in field and event marketing. You’ve built and executed high-impact field and event programs at scale, with a track record of driving real outcomes.

 - Expert understanding of best in class execution of events logistics. You know how to make the trains run on time, and what makes an event really pop.

 - Creative, buyer-obsessed thinking. The best marketing doesn’t blend in. You deeply understand your audience and design experiences that stand out and get talked about.

 - Relentless operational excellence. Great ideas only matter when the execution is flawless. You sweat the details, run tight processes, and ensure every event delivers an incredible experience.

 - Hustle and grit. You thrive in ambiguity, take ownership of new problems, and iterate quickly until you land what works.

 - Exceptional cross-functional skills. You build strong partnerships with Sales and external partners to create programs that are aligned, high leverage, and easy to execute against.

 - High-ownership leader. You set the standard, lead from the front, and take full accountability for outcomes.

 - This role requires significant travel.

What brings us together is our commitment to:

 - Live to build

 - Run through walls and win

 - Obsess over customers in each line of code

 - Lose sleep over the "almost perfect"

 - Show internal locus of control

 - Prioritize finesse: refinement of first principles thinking, execution, and craftsmanship

We are an equal opportunity empl

source_jobs.title: Founding Product Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Netic is the AI revenue engine for essential services who are the backbone of the American economy.

With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.

You’ll join our team with relentless builders from Scale, Databricks, HRT, Meta, MIT, Stanford, and Harvard in bringing frontier AI to the physical economy, where the problems are hard, the data is complex, and the impact is immediate and tangible.

As a Founding Product Infrastructure Engineer, you’ll own and evolve the backbone that powers our autonomous AI agents in production. What makes this role different at Netic is the shape of the problem. You are not building infrastructure for a single product or a single industry. You are building a shared foundation that must support very different workflows and traffic patterns across multiple verticals. Some requests are low latency and real time. Others are long running, stateful, and failure prone. The same system needs to handle spikes, partial failures, and external dependencies without becoming fragile or hard to reason about.

You’ll work closely with product and application engineers to make infrastructure a force multiplier: fast to change, reliable under load, and designed with clear tradeoffs. If you enjoy building systems from first principles, operating them in the real world, and setting standards others will build on, this role is for you.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Define deployment and environment strategy
   Own CI/CD, environment isolation, rollout strategies, and failure recovery. Ensure deploys are boring, reversible, and observable.
   

 - Own databases and performance at scale
   Help determine how our databases should be structured, operated, and evolved. Establish processes around migrations, slow query analysis, connection management, and capacity planning.
   

 - Raise the operational bar
   Define best practices around reliability, security, incident response, and operational hygiene—and help the rest of the team adopt them.
   

 - Plan for growth, not just today
   Make pragmatic decisions for our current stage while clearly mapping how infrastructure will evolve over the next 12–24 months as usage, customers, and workloads scale.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Strong infrastructure foundations
   4+ years operating production systems at scale on a major cloud provider (we use GCP today; AWS or Azure experience is great too).
   

 - Infrastructure automation expertise
   Deep experience with infrastructure-as-code and CI/CD (Terraform, GitHub Actions, or similar), including ownership of real production environments.
   

 - Database and performance judgment
   Hands-on experience running and tuning production databases. You’ve dealt with slow queries, connection limits, migrations, and performance regressions, and built processes to prevent them from recurring.
   

 - Observability fluency
   Practical experience with modern monitoring and tracing tools (we use Datadog), and an opinionated approach to what’s worth measuring versus noise.
   

 - Security and reliability awareness
   Familiarity with IAM, networking, service-to-service authentication, and the realities of operating systems that customers depend on.
   

 - Product-minded infrastructure thinking
   You treat infrastructure as a product: you prioritize well, make tradeoffs explicit, measure impact, and continuously improve rather than chasing theoretical perfection.

What brings us together is our commitment to:

 - Live to build

 - Run through walls and win

 - Obsess over customers in each line of code

 - Lose sleep over the "almost perfect"

 - Show internal locus of control

 - Prioritize finesse: refinement of first principles thinking, execution, and craftsmanship

We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate

source_jobs.title: Founding Web Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Netic is the AI revenue engine for essential services who are the backbone of the American economy.

With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.

You’ll join our team with relentless builders from Scale, Databricks, HRT, Meta, MIT, Stanford, and Harvard in bringing frontier AI to the physical economy, where the problems are hard, the data is complex, and the impact is immediate and tangible.

As a Founding Web Platform Engineer, you will own the frontend platform that every product team at Netic builds on top of. Our Next.js application is already in production and serving customers — what it needs now is real platform structure underneath it: a design system and component library that product engineers reach for by default, app-shell primitives that make new surfaces fast to spin up, and the conventions for data fetching, forms, state, and routing that keep many product teams shipping in the same direction without each reinventing the basics.

What makes this role uniquely rare at Netic is that the platform sits between frontier AI products and the people actually running essential-services businesses. The interfaces you shape are how operators, dispatchers, and technicians experience AI in their daily work — so the abstractions have to be flexible enough for fast product iteration across verticals, and disciplined enough that every screen feels coherent, accessible, and fast. If you are excited to define how a company's product surface scales — from one app today to many product teams shipping confidently on shared foundations — this is an opportunity to build a durable, company-defining platform.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build the Design System and Component Library: Year one, this is the centerpiece. Define the tokens, primitives, and patterns; build a component library product engineers want to use because it's better than rolling their own. Own accessibility, theming, and the contracts that keep the UI coherent as the company grows.

 - Create Frontend Platform Primitives with Leverage: Build the app shell, routing conventions, auth/session handling, typed API clients, data-fetching patterns, form and table frameworks, and the state primitives that product teams compose into features. Your success is measured by how easily other engineers ship powerful AI-driven product surfaces on top of your work.

 - Treat Product Engineers as Customers: Partner closely with product and engineering teams to understand their needs, eliminate friction, and design APIs and components that are intuitive, well-documented, and hard to misuse. Office hours, RFCs, migration guides — whatever it takes to make adoption the easy path.

 - Own Build, Performance, and Client Observability: Take responsibility for the Next.js build pipeline, bundle size, Core Web Vitals, error tracking, client-side logging, and feature-flag/experimentation plumbing. Make intentional tradeoffs around rendering strategy (SSR/RSC/CSR), caching, and code-splitting based on real product needs.

 - Set Frontend Platform Direction: Work with leadership to define the long-term architecture — build-vs-buy decisions, how the design system evolves, when to split into multiple apps or a monorepo, and how the platform scales as product surface area grows.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Frontend Platform Experience: 5+ years building production web applications, with experience owning a design system, component library, or core frontend abstraction used by multiple product teams. Deep React and TypeScript fluency; strong Next.js experience (App Router, RSC, rendering tradeoffs).

 - Design System Craft: You've built or substantially contributed to a real design system — not just a folder of components, but the tokens, primitives, accessibility baseline, documentation, an

source_jobs.title: GTM Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Netic is the AI revenue engine for essential services who are the backbone of the American economy.

With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.

You’ll join our team with relentless builders from Scale, Databricks, HRT, Meta, MIT, Stanford, and Harvard in bringing frontier AI to the physical economy, where the problems are hard, the data is complex, and the impact is immediate and tangible.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a GTM Engineer to set the standard for how we use data and systems to not only build our own GTM engine but also help our customers build theirs.

At Netic, we currently are laying the foundation for the best GTM engine in tech. We need to build the TAM, track signals against it, choose the right set of tools to buy vs. build, set up an environment for all marketers and sellers to effectively leverage AI and much more.

For our customers, we get to do the same.

This is a perfect opportunity for the hungriest of GTM Engineers that cares about building sophisticated systems that have an outsized impact on revenue. If you’re interested, send a 2 minute video of something you’ve built to noah@netic.ai.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Level up how we map the essential service TAM across the US

 - Come up with, test, and monitor the best intent signals against our database

 - Help inform a lot of Netic’s early tech stack

 - Build the foundational internal tools, workflows, and automations for sales and marketing at Netic to use & leverage AI to be more effective

 - Come up with ways to use GTM Engineering principles to help our customers be more successful

 - Build a flywheel based on customer conversations to accelerate our understanding of our users

 - Dig into the Netic customer usage data to inform TOF campaigns and messaging

 - Chip in wherever is needed as we scale the team




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 3+ years in GTM Engineering, sales, growth, RevOps or a MarketingOps role

 - Strong technical foundations – you can write some amount of code yourself and you understand how to set up the right architecture for systems that can scale

 - Great business intuition. You have demonstrated experience moving the needle on the most important metrics you have owned and choosing the right set of initiatives to focus on

 - Deep curiosity. You tinker in your free time and have demonstrated high levels of curiosity + running down rabbit holes.

 - Hustle and grit. You thrive in ambiguity, take ownership of new problems, and iterate quickly until you land what works.

 - Exceptional cross-functional skills. You build strong partnerships with Sales and internal partners to build systems that are aligned, high leverage, and easy to execute against.

 - High-ownership leader. You set the standard, lead from the front, and take full accountability for outcomes.

What brings us together is our commitment to:

 - Live to build

 - Run through walls and win

 - Obsess over customers in each line of code

 - Lose sleep over the "almost perfect"

 - Show internal locus of control

 - Prioritize finesse: refinement of first principles thinking, execution, and craftsmanship

We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.title: Founding Outbound SDR
source_jobs.description:
Netic is the AI revenue engine for essential services who are the backbone of the American economy.

With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.

You’ll join our team with relentless builders from Scale, Databricks, HRT, Meta, MIT, Stanford, and Harvard in bringing frontier AI to the physical economy, where the problems are hard, the data is complex, and the impact is immediate and tangible.


ABOUT THE ROLE

The Founding Outbound Sales Development Representative (SDR) role at Netic offers a unique opportunity to be the first SDR at the company while we scale up all of our GTM efforts. At the core of your role, you will be responsible for generating pipeline.

We are looking for a driven and ambitious SDR who can be very strategic about how you go after our target customers. You will partner closely with Account Executives and Marketing to generate qualified pipeline, contribute directly to Netic’s growth and help inform the underlying system that we build to prospect into our best accounts.

We are looking to build our SDR motion in the most effective way given all of the new tooling at our disposal. This means you will try new tools, share feedback with the GTM Engineering team to level up the underlying brain of our systems and work with a best-in-class GTM org.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build Pipeline. Your most important job is to book meetings with our target accounts. You should do this with deep account research, high quality copy, and creative ideas to stand out. Outbound calling will be crucial.

 - Become an expert on all of Netic’s products and understand the competitive landscape

 - Collaborate with Account Executives and Marketing to design and execute outbound campaigns

 - Prospect via phone, email, and social channels to connect with key decision-makers

 - Maintain CRM accuracy and ensure all prospecting activities are well-documented

 - Hold the bar high for how you represent Netic. We are going after some of the largest, most important essential service businesses in the country. It’s critical that you hold a high bar of professionalism and quality in everything that you do
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 2+ years of experience in a selling or customer-facing role, ideally as an SDR

 - Familiarity using GTM software tools like Clay and Apollo to drive pipeline

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Ability to adapt quickly in a fast-paced, constantly evolving environment

 - Strong results orientation with a proven track record of exceeding activity and pipeline goals

 - Collaborative mindset and openness to feedback and coaching

What brings us together is our commitment to:

 - Live to build

 - Run through walls and win

 - Obsess over customers in each line of code

 - Lose sleep over the "almost perfect"

 - Show internal locus of control

 - Prioritize finesse: refinement of first principles thinking, execution, and craftsmanship

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
Netic is the AI revenue engine for essential services who are the backbone of the American economy.

With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.

You’ll join our team with relentless builders from Scale, Databricks, HRT, Meta, MIT, Stanford, and Harvard in bringing frontier AI to the physical economy, where the problems are hard, the data is complex, and the impact is immediate and tangible.

We’re looking for a product designer (2nd design hire) to shape net-new interactions between humans and AI, build core product, and set the foundation to scale the entire design team.

What you’ll do

 - Own end to end: work with engineers and agent PMs to deeply understand the problem, explore solutions, execute, ship, absorb feedback, and iterate. You're accountable for outcomes.

 - Embrace innovation and change: pair with AI to clarify your thinking, stress-test edge cases, and validate ideas fast. Experiment with and build high-leverage systems for your own design process using AI.

 - Figure it out: talk to customers, find the person with the answer, run your own experiments. Do whatever it takes to get the information you need with speed and precision.

 - Build the full picture: prototype initial designs with Cursor / Claude to validate ideas and interaction patterns quickly; refine the craft in Figma. Think → prototype in code → validate → polish.

 - Build the design system: develop and maintain a flexible design system that ensures efficiency, visual cohesion, and smooth integration with engineering workflows. The system should make the team faster and more consistent than ever.

 - Be opinionated: develop strong views on tools, prompts, methods, our product, and how we work. Challenge the team on assumed truths. Test and experiment ideas and share learnings. Push the direction. Design is an enabler; never a blocker.

What you’ll need

 - Proven experience: you've designed and shipped something complex. You've had to make real decisions with real tradeoffs, not just execute someone else's spec.

 - Ownership: you create your own direction, form your own opinions, and figure out how to deliver without waiting for permission or perfect information.

 - Communication: crisp, honest, direct. You write well, present well, and know when to be brief.

 - Team player: great collaborator and mediator. Navigate different stakeholders (eng, product, ops, customers, leadership) with cohesion and clarity.

 - AI fluent: you're fluent in AI tools and actively applying them to your design process with intention.

Nice to haves

 - Experience building and maintaining design systems.

 - Front-end code proficiency.

 - Experience conducting customer interviews and user research.

What brings us together is our commitment to:

 - Live to build

 - Run through walls and win

 - Obsess over customers in each line of code

 - Lose sleep over the "almost perfect"

 - Show internal locus of control

 - Prioritize finesse: refinement of first principles thinking, execution, and craftsmanship

We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.description:
Netic is the AI revenue engine for essential services who are the backbone of the American economy.

With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.

You’ll join our team with relentless builders from Scale, Databricks, HRT, Meta, MIT, Stanford, and Harvard in bringing frontier AI to the physical economy, where the problems are hard, the data is complex, and the impact is immediate and tangible.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Netic serves some of the largest, most important essential service businesses in the US, and we are in the process of serving many more.

We are looking for a creative and results oriented growth marketing generalist that will run campaigns end to end and help us break into our highest priority accounts. You will come up with and execute on memorable, A+ quality campaigns.

You will work closely with our sales org to source ideas, gather context from the front lines and execute on campaign follow-through.

This is a perfect role for a results oriented and creative marketer that wants to go big on their ideas and stand out with some of the biggest brands in the country.

 - Deeply understand our customers in order to find ways to cut through the noise

 - Get creative with how we use AI and our internal data to run maximally effective campaigns

 - Creativity. You are naturally creative and have a history of coming up with creative ideas to cut through the noise.

With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.

You’ll join our team with relentless builders from Scale, Databricks, HRT, Meta, MIT, Stanford, and Harvard in bringing frontier AI to the physical economy, where the problems are hard, the data is complex, and the impact is immediate and tangible.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Netic serves some of the largest, most important essential service businesses in the US, and we are in the process of serving many more.

We are looking for an experienced, detail oriented, and analytical Founding RevOps to support our growing sales org and GTM systems. You will be our Salesforce admin, build out our sales systems, sales strategy, forecasting muscle and more.

This role will shape how we think about RevOps and our entire sales motion at Netic. If you want to be the founding RevOps hire at a company with strong PMF and a quickly growing sales org, this is a good fit for you.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own and optimize the end-to-end revenue operations stack (e.g., CRM, forecasting, pipeline management, tooling).

 - Serve as our Salesforce admin - improving the quality and effectiveness of our CRM over time.

 - Ensure data integrity, reporting accuracy, and scalable processes across the revenue lifecycle. You should be using AI to help with this.

 - Identify gaps and inefficiencies in the GTM funnel and then work with sales, growth and GTM Engineering to solve them.

 - Lead revenue forecasting, pipeline health, and performance reporting.

 - Support territory design, capacity planning, quota setting, and incentive structures.

 - Enable revenue teams with clear processes, documentation, and operational clarity.

 - Establish clear operating cadences, documentation, and performance reviews and MBRs for the BU and Sales Leadership.
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Proven experience scaling revenue operations in a growth-stage company. You’ve seen and played a big part in helping sales orgs grow in the past.

 - Strong analytical mindset. Comfortable working with data, models, and metrics.

 - Experience with quotas. You have experience setting and handling SDR quotas.

 - Meaningful experience with Salesforce. You don’t have to have Salesforce admin experience, but you should understand how to get anything done in Salesforce, and ideally have connections with Salesforce developers if we need them.

source_jobs.title: Technical Recruiter - New Grad
source_jobs.description:
Netic is the AI revenue engine for essential services who are the backbone of the American economy.

With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.

You’ll join our team with relentless builders from Scale, Databricks, HRT, Meta, MIT, Stanford, and Harvard in bringing frontier AI to the physical economy, where the problems are hard, the data is complex, and the impact is immediate and tangible.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Netic is growing fast and tackling a massive, underserved market. This role is a rare opportunity for a highly ambitious recent graduate to break into the startup ecosystem and learn the art of recruiting from the ground up. You will work side-by-side with our founders, learn how top-tier engineering and go-to-market teams are built, and experience early-stage startup growth firsthand. If you want a fast-paced launchpad into recruiting, venture capital, or startup operations, this is the place to start.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Find and talk to the sharpest software engineers, PMs, and GTM leaders from top-tier universities and companies. You'll own the outreach strategy from day one.

 - Learn the full hiring lifecycle, which means everything from writing your own outbound pitches and running initial screens to managing the candidate pipeline and helping close offers.

 - Figure out ways to use AI and automation tools to handle the boring stuff so you can focus on writing better messages and talking to candidates.

 - Partner directly with our internal teams to calibrate on active roles and use data to spot and fix bottlenecks in our hiring process.

 - Give every single candidate an incredible experience and get really good at telling the Netic story.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - A recent or upcoming degree from a top school, but more importantly, a habit of working hard and winning at things you care about (whether that’s academics, sports, or projects you built outside of class).

 - Real grit. We move fast and things change quickly here. You need to be comfortable with ambiguity and have a natural drive to run through walls to get things done.

 - Strong communication. You should love writing, speak clearly, and be confident holding your own in conversations with hyper-technical or highly experienced people.

 - An analytical mindset. Recruiting is a numbers game. You care about response rates, conversion loops, and using data to improve your process.

 - A high slope. You don’t need a background in recruiting, but you do need to learn technical concepts quickly, take feedback well, and adapt on the fly.

What brings us together is our commitment to:

 - Live to build

 - Run through walls and win

 - Obsess over customers in each line of code

 - Lose sleep over the "almost perfect"

 - Show internal locus of control

 - Prioritize finesse: refinement of first principles thinking, execution, and craftsmanship

We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.
